# Implementation Plan: Dynamic Traffic Generation & Simulation Adaptability

**Branch**: `005-dynamic-traffic-generation-simulation-adaptability` | **Date**: 2026-05-01 | **Spec**: [`specs/005-dynamic-traffic-generation-simulation-adaptability/spec.md`](specs/005-dynamic-traffic-generation-simulation-adaptability/spec.md)
**Input**: Feature specification from `/specs/005-dynamic-traffic-generation-simulation-adaptability/spec.md`

**Note**: This plan is generated by the `/speckit.plan` workflow and continues the completed environment boundary from feature 004 without changing lifecycle ownership.

## Summary

Implement a paper-backed, seeded Bernoulli traffic substrate that produces deterministic per-agent, per-slot task arrivals for the existing `HoodieGymEnvironment`, while leaving `reset(seed)` / `step(action)` semantics, delayed reward timing, and `SlotEngine` helper-only boundary unchanged. The feature adds traffic presets, traffic trace generation, and traffic observability metrics that are compatible with the existing `EvaluationTrace` / `TraceTaskBlueprint` path.

## Technical Context

**Language/Version**: Python 3.14.3 in the workspace; repository code is Python and uses modern dataclass/typing features  
**Primary Dependencies**: Standard library only for this feature; existing repo modules under `src/environment` and `src/evaluation`; no new external packages  
**Storage**: File-backed specs, docs, and optional JSON trace payloads; no database  
**Testing**: Existing `unittest`-based unit and integration tests plus new traffic-generation regression coverage  
**Target Platform**: Local development and Linux-compatible Python runtime  
**Project Type**: Research reproduction simulator / library-style package with environment and evaluation runners  
**Performance Goals**: Deterministic trace generation should remain linear in `episode_length × number_of_agents` and must not add noticeable overhead to environment stepping  
**Constraints**: No dependency changes, no Gymnasium/ns-3/ns-3-gym, no TorchRL/training/agent/neural-network changes, no lifecycle ownership changes, no hidden traffic-model switching  
**Scale/Scope**: Single repository feature that adds traffic generation and observability surfaces only

## Project Structure

### Documentation (this feature)

```text
specs/005-dynamic-traffic-generation-simulation-adaptability/
├── plan.md
├── research.md
├── data-model.md
├── quickstart.md
├── contracts/
└── tasks.md
```

### Source Code (repository root)

```text
src/
├── environment/
│   ├── traffic_config.py
│   ├── traffic_generator.py
│   ├── traffic_observer.py
│   ├── gym_adapter.py
│   ├── trace_source.py
│   ├── task.py
│   ├── topology.py
│   ├── runtime_model.py
│   └── slot_engine.py
├── evaluation/
│   ├── trace_protocol.py
│   ├── runner.py
│   └── validation_runner.py
└── policies/

tests/
├── unit/
└── integration/
```

**Structure Decision**: Keep traffic configuration, generation, and observability in `src/environment/`, keep `src/evaluation/trace_protocol.py` as the compatibility carrier for `EvaluationTrace` / `TraceTaskBlueprint`, and do not introduce a new simulator package, training-layer dependency, or lifecycle controller. `HoodieGymEnvironment` remains the only lifecycle orchestrator and `SlotEngine` remains helper-only.
